summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal satisfied (no new tables, modifies existing cid_intake + replaces fn_promote_emission within lcs schema, cantonal pattern intact), Registry-First (N/A — no tables created), Sovereign ID (no CL minting, hardening existing promotion path), No sideways calls (same read pattern as SRC3, writes only within lcs). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=fix, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — all 7 acceptance criteria met. emission_id UUID column added to cid_intake with partial unique index WHERE emission_id IS NOT NULL (nullable is valid adaptation — non-promotion CIDs have no emission_id). Backfill from source_record_id for UUID-formatted values. fn_promote_emission replaced with idempotent version: already-PROMOTED check returns clean (replay safety), pre-check SELECT for existing CID before INSERT (conflict safety), three-layer defense against double-minting (FOR UPDATE row lock + pre-check SELECT + partial unique index). No RAISE on any path (SRC3 correctness preserved). Batch wrapper unchanged (already uses FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + return-state logic). 9 enforcement tests across 5 categories (cid_intake uniqueness, idempotent promotion, emission_id in CID, batch concurrency, forward-only status). OBSERVATIONS: (1) Nullable emission_id instead of WP's NOT NULL — correct for backwards compatibility with non-promotion CIDs. (2) Pre-check SELECT instead of WP's ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING — equivalent safety via FOR UPDATE lock, cleaner approach. (3) Three-layer defense: row lock prevents concurrent promotion, pre-check catches replay, unique index is database-level backstop. (4) Forward-only status enforcement is behavioral (function-level), not trigger-enforced — status column remains mutable per SRC4 guard. (5) Dependencies satisfied: Phase 2A PASS, SRC3 PASS, SRC4 PASS.
